seoTitle: Bongaree vs Bonnie Doon — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Bongaree and Bonnie Doon. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Bongaree vs Bonnie Doon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bongaree and Bonnie Doon.
heading: Bongaree vs Bonnie Doon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bongaree, Queensland and Bonnie Doon, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bonnie Doon has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Bongaree (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bongaree or Bonnie Doon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bongaree has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Bonnie Doon scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bongaree scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Bonnie Doon scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bongaree or Bonnie Doon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bonnie Doon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.82% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
